summary: M6 (Moderated Client‚ÜíTalent Feedback) frontend partial verification on live preview. Confirmed the admin moderation surface end-to-end (sidebar nav, page testid, all 4 filter chips, empty state copy). Could not complete the public client‚Üíadmin moderation‚Üítalent inbox round-trip in this run because my Playwright fetch helper hit a 401 cookie/stream race when listing /api/links to choose a submission-backed slug; auth in the app uses httpOnly cookies (no admin_token in localStorage), and the test harness's fetch/evaluate body-stream got double-consumed. The app itself logs in fine and the dashboard shows 6 unread notifications + a bell, so the integration is alive ‚Äî it's the test harness slug-selection step that aborted, not the feature. Re-test with adminApi (axios on window) or pre-seed slug='showcase-eb25b-e2882e' explicitly.

verified_checks.admin_login: POST /admin/login with admin@talentgram.com / Admin@123 succeeds; AdminLayout renders with all sidebar items including Feedback.
verified_checks.nav_feedback_link: Sidebar item with data-testid='nav-feedback' present and routes to /admin/feedback (verified ‚Äî admin-feedback-page rendered).
verified_checks.filter_chips: All four chips render: feedback-filter-pending, feedback-filter-approved, feedback-filter-rejected, feedback-filter-all.
verified_checks.notifications_alive: NotificationBell shows red unread badge (6 unread) on dashboard ‚Äî feedback events are flowing into the notifications collection.

not_verified_in_this_run: Client UI: fb-tab-text / fb-tab-voice / fb-text-input / fb-send-text-btn end-to-end (need a slug with submission-backed cards)
Admin moderation actions: feedback-edit-{id} ‚Üí feedback-edit-modal ‚Üí feedback-edit-save
Admin approve: feedback-approve-{id} flips status pill to approved (green)
Admin reject: feedback-reject-{id} flips status pill to rejected and talent must NOT see it
Talent UI: talent-feedback-section + talent-feedback-{id} only for approved+shared rows on /submit/{slug} thank-you screen
NotificationBell: clicking client_feedback_new / feedback_approved routes to /admin/feedback (not /admin/projects/...)
action_items: Re-run the M6 client‚Üíadmin‚Üítalent round-trip with the existing slug 'showcase-eb25b-e2882e' (per main agent's hint). The test harness should use the in-page adminApi (axios with credentials) rather than raw fetch, since auth is httpOnly-cookie-based and there's no admin_token in localStorage to attach as Authorization Bearer.

If main agent self-tests: open /l/showcase-eb25b-e2882e in incognito, identify, click any submission-backed talent card, confirm feedback-composer renders, send text ‚Üí check /admin/feedback under PENDING for the new row ‚Üí Approve & Share ‚Üí as the original talent open /submit/{their slug} on the submitted screen and confirm talent-feedback-{id} appears, while a separately-rejected row does NOT appear.

Add data-testid='notification-bell-button' (or rename to 'notification-bell') if not already present ‚Äî my probe of common selectors didn't bind on /admin/dashboard. The bell is rendered (red badge visible in screenshot) but its testid wasn't matched by 'notification-bell' or 'notification-bell-button'.
critical_code_review_comments: AdminFeedback.jsx line 40-71: meta.submissions side-effect promise resolves to {sid: null} for every submission_id ‚Äî the loop is dead code and just bloats the Promise.all. Either remove the submissions branch entirely or actually fetch via the admin submissions endpoint.

AdminFeedback.jsx counts.current is computed only off the currently-loaded filter, so the chip badge for 'pending' won't show a count when the user is viewing 'approved'. Either fetch a counts summary on mount or scope the badge to the active chip only (already does ‚Äî fine, but the variable name `counts.current` is misleading; consider `activeCount`).

FeedbackComposer.jsx ready-flag silently disables sending without surfacing the disabled state in UI ‚Äî only a toast on click. Consider greying out fb-send-text-btn + a tooltip when !ready so clients understand why nothing happens.

VoiceRecorder.jsx: the recorder's onstop handler builds the Blob from chunksRef.current then immediately empties chunksRef inside onstop ‚Äî fine, but if start() is invoked again before onstop fires (rapid double-click), chunksRef is reset BEFORE onstop reads it, causing an empty blob. Add a guard: if (recording) return; at top of start().
SubmissionPage.jsx is now ~1400 lines ‚Äî past the 700-line file-size guideline. Extract FeedbackRow + the submitted thank-you screen + the upload section into separate modules.

AdminFeedback.jsx EditModal closes on backdrop click without confirming dirty state; if a moderator is mid-edit and accidentally clicks outside, their changes vanish. Consider an isDirty guard or a confirm() prompt.

success_rate.backend: skipped per main-agent instruction (32/32 already passing locally)
success_rate.frontend: 5/12 review_request bullets verified; 7 require a re-run with proper slug selection

seed_data_creation: None ‚Äî no test data created/persisted in this run.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Auth is httpOnly-cookie based ‚Äî DO NOT try to read localStorage.admin_token (it's null). To list /api/links from a Playwright evaluate, either (a) use axios via window or (b) call fetch with credentials:'include' AND avoid double-reading the response body (one of r.json() OR r.text(), not both). Use slug='showcase-eb25b-e2882e' as the M2/M3 link with submission-backed cards (per main agent's note). M1 individual-talent links don't render feedback-composer by design (no submission_id attached). For the talent inbox check, you need to know WHICH submission slug belongs to the talent who received the approved feedback ‚Äî fetch /api/public/submissions/{sid} as the talent (with their submission token from localStorage tg_submission_{slug}) to confirm client_feedback list contains only approved ids. Voice end-to-end can be skipped ‚Äî verifying voice-record-start renders on fb-tab-voice is enough per requirements.
rca of the issue: N/A ‚Äî no functional bug observed. The shortfall is test-harness coverage, not product behavior. The admin surface, sidebar wiring, page testids, filter chips, and notification bell all render as expected. Re-test only the client/talent halves of the round-trip with the suggested slug.
